metadata.title: grown-workspace - System Security Plan (SKELETON)

roles.title: Authorizing Official (NONE — system is not authorized)

metadata.remarks: STRONG SKELETON, not exhaustive. Covers the controls grown actually addresses; the full FedRAMP Moderate baseline (~325 controls) is NOT fully enumerated here. UNVALIDATED against an OSCAL validator. The system has NO authorizing official and NO ATO.

import-profile.remarks: Resolves to the GSA FedRAMP Rev 5 Moderate baseline profile (which in turn imports the NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 catalog). A second profile, ./profiles/nist-800-171.json, covers CMMC L2 / 800-171; only one import-profile is allowed per SSP, so the 171 mapping is documented in compliance/cmmc-l2-mapping.md and via control-171 props in the component-definition.

system-characteristics.description: grown-workspace is a self-hosted, multi-tenant productivity suite (drive, docs, sheets, slides, mail, chat, meet, calendar, contacts, photos, books, music, video, projects, forms, sites, keep, groups, whiteboards) plus a document-signing app (PDF/pdf). It is a single Go gRPC + grpc-gateway backend that also serves a React SPA, fronted by a Zitadel OIDC identity provider, backed by PostgreSQL and an S3-compatible blob store, deployed via GitOps (Flux) on a Talos/Kubernetes cluster with TLS terminated by cert-manager at a Gateway API edge.

information-types.title: User-generated productivity content
information-types.description: Documents, spreadsheets, slides, files, photos, media, and notes created/uploaded by org members. Stored in Postgres + the blob store, scoped by org_id.

information-types.title: Identity & authentication data
information-types.description: User identifiers (OIDC subject/issuer, email, display name) in grown.users and opaque session tokens in grown.sessions. Passwords/MFA secrets live in Zitadel, NOT grown.

information-types.title: Audit & accountability records
information-types.description: grown.audit_events: append-only activity trail of every mutating action across all services, with actor/IP/method/status.

status.remarks: NOT operational under any authorization. Homelab/development system with no ATO, no FedRAMP authorization, and no CMMC certification. No federal data (FCI/CUI) is processed.

authorization-boundary.description: The authorization boundary comprises: (1) the grown-workspace container (Go backend + embedded React SPA + migrations), (2) the PostgreSQL instance holding all per-tenant data, sessions, and audit records, (3) the RustFS/S3 blob store for user binaries, (4) the Zitadel OIDC provider (external identity authority, a trusted upstream within the same cluster), and (5) the Gateway API / cert-manager TLS edge. Build and deploy are governed by a GitOps (Flux) pipeline with SOPS+age-encrypted secrets. Excluded/inherited: the Talos/Kubernetes cluster, host OS, physical/network infrastructure, and Cloudflare edge are INHERITED from the pick-gitops platform and not re-documented here. Multi-tenant isolation is logical (org_id scoping in a shared Postgres/blob store), not physical; in practice the system runs single-org today (multi-org request routing is stubbed in internal/tenancy).

users.title: Org member
users.remarks: Authenticated end user. grown has no formal role model; 'admin' authority is conferred by membership in the GROWN_ADMIN_EMAILS env allowlist, not a stored role.

users.title: Org admin (allowlist)
users.remarks: An org member whose email is in GROWN_ADMIN_EMAILS. Can write per-org service settings, view the audit log, and manage users. When the allowlist is empty, admin writes fall open to any member — see POA&M poam-rbac.

components.title: Auth / Zitadel OIDC Integration
components.description: See component-definition.json (same UUID). Authentication, sessions, in-app MFA proxy.

components.title: Audit Subsystem
components.description: See component-definition.json (same UUID). Cross-cutting audit trail.

components.title: PostgreSQL (per-tenant data store)
components.description: See component-definition.json (same UUID). Org-scoped data store.

components.title: Blob Store (RustFS / S3-compatible)
components.description: See component-definition.json (same UUID). User binary storage.

components.title: Gateway / TLS Termination (cert-manager + Gateway API)
components.description: See component-definition.json (same UUID). TLS edge, inherited.

control-implementation.description: Per-control implementation statements for the FedRAMP Moderate (800-53 Rev 5) controls grown addresses. Each requirement points at the implementing component (by-component) defined in component-definition.json. This is a representative, NON-exhaustive set; controls not listed here are either inherited, not-applicable, or unmet (see poam.json).

by-components.description: Auth/Zitadel: all requests authenticated via OIDC session before any state mutation (auth.HTTPMiddleware).
MFA (TOTP/passkeys) is available via the Zitadel proxy but NOT enforced org-wide. Gap tracked in poam-mfa.
Authenticator management delegated to Zitadel via the self-scoped User API v2 proxy; users manage only their own authenticators.
Postgres: every table org_id-scoped; repositories filter by caller org. Logical multi-tenant isolation.
Auth resolves user+org into context for every request; admin-gated surfaces enforce the allowlist.
Opaque sessions with expires_at/revoked_at; Lookup rejects expired/revoked; Logout revokes. (session.go, service.go)
Audit interceptor + HTTP middleware capture every mutating RPC and binary route across all services.
Each event records when/who/what/where/outcome (timestamp, actor, service+action+method, ip/ua, status). (migration 0037, repository.go)
Single central audit-generation capability wired in server.go covers all services; cannot drift per-service.
TLS terminated at the Gateway (cert-manager + Let's Encrypt); cookies flagged Secure. Intra-cluster hop unverified; see poam-boundary.
AES-256-GCM KEK for PDF signing keys + TLS; NONE FIPS 140-validated. Gap tracked in poam-fips.
Declarative baseline in git + pinned image, reconciled by Flux; schema baseline is the ordered migration set.
